portfolio_summary.macro_summary: MIXED regime with elevated caution signals: SPY RSI 73 (overbought), VIX 17.19 (contained but not complacent), Fear & Greed at 38 (fear) — a rare divergence where price momentum is extended while sentiment lags. 10Y yield at 4.36% continues to pressure growth multiples. The SPY RSI reading is the primary near-term risk: historically, corrections of 5–10% follow SPY RSI>70 within 4–6 weeks. Trim overbought tech into strength; avoid new high-beta longs until RSI normalizes below 65.

cross_position_insights.description: GOOG and GOOGL represent duplicate exposure to Alphabet Inc. with a combined portfolio value of approximately $3,203 (6.2% of total portfolio). Both positions carry RSI readings of 83-84, amplifying the concentration risk during an overbought market environment.
cross_position_insights.recommendation: Sell the single GOOG share ($397) and trim 2-3 of 7 GOOGL shares near $398. Consolidate into one vehicle. This keeps Google thesis intact while reducing RSI-driven reversion risk.

cross_position_insights.description: Five positions have RSI readings above 80 simultaneously while SPY RSI is 73: INTC (85.1), FTNT (84.4), GOOGL (84.0), GOOG (83.3), AMD (80.8). This cluster of extreme readings in a broadly overbought market creates systemic reversion risk. Historically, clusters of RSI>80 positions in an overbought market precede 5-15% corrections in affected names within 2-4 weeks.
cross_position_insights.recommendation: Execute trims on INTC (60-75%), FTNT (50%), and AMD (30-40%) within the next 2-3 trading sessions. Partial trims on GOOG and GOOGL for concentration management. Trim into strength — do not wait for RSI to peak.

cross_position_insights.description: Both energy positions (XOM and OXY) are in confirmed downtrends with RSI readings of 37.1 and 37.2. XOM is -6.3% and OXY is -12.5% over 7 days, indicating oil sector broad weakness. Combined energy exposure is approximately $2,779 (5.4% of portfolio).
cross_position_insights.recommendation: Hold both with strict stops ($135 XOM, $48 OXY) and do not add. Monitor WTI crude — if it breaks below $65, consider reducing OXY first. Both have significant analyst upside if oil stabilizes.

cross_position_insights.description: BIDU and BABA represent combined exposure of approximately $1,965 to Chinese ADRs. Both face permanent delisting risk from SEC PCAOB audit requirements and geopolitical escalation risk.
cross_position_insights.recommendation: Combined position size is acceptable at ~3.8% of portfolio. Do not add without geopolitical clarity. Set strict stops ($128 BIDU, $125 BABA). If PCAOB audit status deteriorates, reduce both immediately.

These are either in losses or underperforming but represent significant potential value if their turnaround theses execute. Combined exposure is approximately $9,200 (17.8% of portfolio).
cross_position_insights.recommendation: Maintain META and MSFT as conviction holds. Monitor CRM's Agentforce catalyst closely. Hold NKE and LULU with strict stops. Exit NVO given minimal analyst upside vs the rest of this group.

cross_position_insights.description: Holding VOO (S&P 500 ETF) while also directly owning NVDA, MSFT, AMZN, META, GOOGL, and GOOG creates significant double exposure to top VOO holdings. These 6 names represent ~30% of VOO's weighting, amplifying megacap concentration.
cross_position_insights.recommendation: Acceptable structure for a concentrated growth portfolio. Be aware that a megacap tech drawdown would impact this portfolio more severely than VOO's diversification implies. No immediate action required.

warnings.message: 5 positions have RSI above 80 (INTC 85.1, FTNT 84.4, GOOGL 84.0, GOOG 83.3, AMD 80.8) while SPY RSI is 73 (overbought). This is a rare simultaneous extreme reading. Historically, clusters of RSI>80 in overbought markets resolve with 10-20% corrections within 2-4 weeks. Trim these positions into strength before the reversal.

3 positions are trading ABOVE analyst consensus targets: INTC ($124.92 vs $80.93 target, -35.2%), FTNT ($114.07 vs $95.69, -16.1%), TSLA ($428.35 vs $412.25, -3.8%). When price exceeds analyst consensus, risk/reward is negative. This premium is vulnerable to mean reversion.

SPY RSI at 73 signals the broad market is overbought. Fear & Greed at 38 (Fear) creates an unusual divergence: price momentum is extended while sentiment hasn't caught up. This combination often precedes a 5-8% index correction. Raise cash by trimming overbought winners (INTC, FTNT, AMD). Avoid new high-beta long positions until SPY RSI resets below 60.

MPW (138 shares) has no market data and may be delisted. Verify immediately with your broker: (1) Does the position show a price or $0.00? (2) Has it transferred OTC? (3) Were you notified of corporate actions? If delisted and worthless, file for capital loss documentation.

XOM (-6.3% 7d, RSI 37) and OXY (-12.5% 7d, RSI 37) are both in confirmed downtrends. Oil sector weakness may persist if OPEC+ increases production or recession fears spike. Both are above stop-loss levels but require monitoring. Do not add to downtrending positions.

4 positions are in significant losses: NKE (-40.4%), LULU (-36.1%), NVO (-29.5%), CRM (-23.9%). Combined unrealized loss is approximately -$11,200. NVO should be exited (minimal analyst upside). NKE, LULU, CRM have credible recovery theses but require strict stop management.

Combined China ADR exposure (BIDU + BABA) is approximately $1,965 (~3.8% of portfolio). Both face PCAOB audit compliance deadlines and geopolitical escalation risk. Position sizes are acceptable but should not be increased.

10Y yield at 4.36% creates headwind for rate-sensitive positions: PLD (REIT), HD (housing), SOFI (fintech lending), AAL (high debt), RIVN (cash burn). A rate spike above 4.75% would pressure these names disproportionately.
